mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding aggregate income and consumption, driving corporate earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:December 2005 payrolls at 134468 thousand showing steady expansion
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following months with positive PAYEMS momentum do not exceed baseline positive rate of 65.5% by at least 5 percentage points over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.5% restrains credit creation and compresses valuation multiples; once FEDFUNDS exceeds this threshold, subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns turn negative as tightening propagates through corporate borrowing costs.
evidence_refs: fact:December 2005 FEDFUNDS at 4.16% approaching restrictive territory
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rising unemployment (UNRATE) and falling industrial production (INDPRO) jointly signal a contractionary gap where labor and supply weakness reinforce each other, generating negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases over prior month AND INDPRO decreases over prior month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.9% as of December 2005, INDPRO at 108.96 as of November 2005
failure_condition: Joint occurrence of rising UNRATE and falling INDPRO fails to produce negative 20-day returns in > 55% of instances, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) over main effects in a 3-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, declining housing starts (HOUST) foreshadow reduced construction activity and durables demand, leading to negative 5-day S&P 500 returns as the market reprices growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:November 2005 HOUST at 2123 thousand, down from peak levels
failure_condition: In periods classified as late-cycle expansion, HOUST declines no longer predict negative 5-day returns (directional accuracy < 55%) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) combined with a flat or rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicate a negative real policy shock that erodes purchasing power without monetary accommodation, producing negative 60-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change > 3.5% AND FEDFUNDS unchanged or higher
evidence_refs: fact:November 2005 CPI at 197.8, Fed funds at 4.16%
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to generate negative 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered months, or the interaction term's t-statistic falls below 1.5 in a 3-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) momentum serves as a coincident indicator of operating leverage and revenue growth for the corporate sector; positive monthly changes in INDPRO predict positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Positive monthly changes in INDPRO coincide with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of months over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth above trend signals robust consumer spending that translates into immediate earnings surprises, generating positive 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Months with RSAFS growth > 0.5% do not produce positive 5-day returns in at least 60% of triggered instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A falling unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 5% signals a tightening labor market that eventually constrains margins via wage pressure, causing negative 60-day S&P 500 returns as the market anticipates Fed intervention and cost erosion.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 5.0% and declining over prior 3 months
failure_condition: Periods with UNRATE < 5% and declining fail to produce negative 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered quarters, or cumulative return over triggered periods is positive.
